title: agrostis mackliniae
intro: Agrostis mackliniae, also known as sweet bent or bent grass, is a type of grass native to New Zealand and Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Agrostis mackliniae include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In terms of herbal preparations, Agrostis mackliniae is often made into a tea or infusion, although its use as a medicinal plant is largely limited to traditional and local applications.
